verbGlossary.ABUTS: One site improvement {target} meets or adjoins another at a common edge or transition strip
verbGlossary.ANCHORS TO: An enclosure {target} is mechanically fastened to a structural embed or connection point for gravity and lateral load transfer
verbGlossary.BALANCES: {source} adjusts airflow or water flow rates within a distribution {target} to match design specifications
verbGlossary.BONDS: Grounding {target} provides an electrical bonding path to a distribution element for fault protection and code compliance
verbGlossary.CONNECTS TO: One utility or distribution {target} ties into another at a junction point, coupling, or termination
verbGlossary.CONTROLS: {source}controls {target} sends signals to regulate the operation of mechanical or electrical equipment
verbGlossary.COORDINATES WITH: Two {target}s that occupy the same ceiling plane and must be spatially coordinated during layout to avoid conflicts
verbGlossary.DRAINS: A waste or drainage {target} carries fluid away from a fixture or collection point by gravity
verbGlossary.DRAINS INTO: An upper drainage {target} discharges into a lower drainage element through a vertical conductor connection
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} machinery provides the motive force that moves the elevator car through the hoistway
verbGlossary.FEEDS: An upstream distribution {target} delivers a fluid, gas, or medium to a downstream element or piece of equipment
verbGlossary.FRAMES: {source}provides the structural skeleton that a board or {target} element is fastened to
verbGlossary.JOINS: A connection {target} (bolts, welds, embed plates) structurally ties two primary structural members together
verbGlossary.POWERS: An electrical distribution {target} supplies electrical energy to operate equipment, devices, or fixtures
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: {source}must be substantially complete before the next earthwork activity can begin in the same area
verbGlossary.PRESSURIZES: {source} {target} boosts system pressure in a distribution main to meet flow and pressure requirements
verbGlossary.PROTECTS: {source}control {target} safeguards an adjacent work area from soil loss, water intrusion, or sediment runoff
verbGlossary.RECEIVES: {target} element (drywall, casework) accepts a finish material (paint, tile, countertop) applied to its surface
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: One earthwork {target} necessitates the operation of another element when site conditions (e.g., high water table) are present
verbGlossary.ROUTES: {source} {target} provides the physical pathway through which wiring or cabling is pulled
verbGlossary.SEALS: A waterproofing, flashing, or membrane {target} creates a watertight seal at a joint, penetration, or termination
verbGlossary.SECURES: {source}control or security {target} monitors and restricts passage through a door or entry point
verbGlossary.SUPPORTS: {source} bears the gravity and lateral loads of {target} above it in the load path
verbGlossary.TERMINATES AT: {source} {target} ends at a flashing or edge condition that provides a weathertight transition
verbGlossary.TIES: {source} or {target} connects two foundation elements together to distribute loads and resist differential settlement
verbGlossary.UNDERLAYS: A barrier, insulation, or {target} layer is installed behind or beneath an outer finish element as part of a layered wall or roof assembly
verbGlossary.WRAPS: {source} {target} is applied around the exterior of piping or ductwork for thermal or acoustic performance
